messages.content:
Create a retrieval-oriented summary for this parent section of a document.
Use the child summaries below. Mention the topics, entities, dates, tables,
figures, definitions, procedures, claims, metrics, and evidence types covered
when they are visible. Do not invent facts. Return only the summary text.

Parent:
{
  "node_id": "0167",
  "title": "二、和社区玩在一起：竞技类游戏的 “玩家共创式” 品牌运营新范式",
  "page_index": 96,
  "page_range": "96"
}

Child summaries:
[
  {
    "node_id": "0168",
    "title": "1、搭建双向沟通桥梁：在社媒搭建玩家共创阵地，收集优化反馈，邀请核心玩家参与共创，同步建议落地成效，推动玩家转为品牌共建者。",
    "page_range": "96",
    "summary": "This section outlines a player engagement strategy and can answer retrieval queries regarding:\n- **Topics & Procedures:** How to establish a two-way communication bridge on social media, including the workflow of setting up a co-creation space, collecting optimization feedback, inviting core players to participate, and reporting back on the implementation status of their suggestions.\n- **Entities:** Social media platforms, general players, core players, and the brand.\n- **Claims:** Maintaining transparent feedback loops and actively involving core players converts the player base into active brand co-creators.\n- **Definitions:** Implicitly defines a \"player co-creation space\" as a social media-based channel designed for bidirectional communication and collaborative brand/product development.\n- **Dates, Metrics, Tables, Figures, Evidence Types:** None are provided in this section."
  },
  {
    "node_id": "0169",
    "title": "2、打通社区与赛事生态联动：联动社媒发起玩法创作赛事，将优质 UGC 纳入官方矩阵与赛事，吸纳高玩参与二创传播，强化玩家认同与社群粘性。",
    "page_range": "96-97",
    "summary": "This section can answer retrieval questions regarding: **Topics & Procedures:** Strategies for linking gaming communities with tournament ecosystems, specifically launching social media-driven gameplay creation contests, curating high-quality UGC for integration into official channels and competitive events, and recruiting high-skill players (高玩) to produce and disseminate secondary content. **Entities:** Social media platforms, official content matrices, skilled players, UGC creators, and the broader player community. **Claims/Objectives:** The initiative asserts that these linkage strategies will strengthen player identification with the brand/game and enhance community stickiness. **Missing Elements:** The section does not contain specific dates, formal definitions, quantitative metrics, tables, figures, or cited evidence types."
  }
]

message.content:
This section outlines a "player co-creation" brand operation paradigm for competitive games, detailing strategies to integrate player communities into brand development and marketing. It addresses retrieval queries regarding:
- **Topics & Procedures:** Establishing bidirectional social media communication channels to collect optimization feedback, invite core players into co-creation workflows, and report back on suggestion implementation; and linking community ecosystems with competitive tournaments by launching social media-driven gameplay creation contests, curating high-quality UGC for integration into official channels and events, and recruiting skilled players to produce and disseminate secondary content.
- **Entities:** Social media platforms, general/core/skilled players, UGC creators, official content matrices, tournament ecosystems, and the game brand.
- **Claims:** Maintaining transparent feedback loops and actively involving core players converts the player base into brand co-creators, while integrating community activities with tournament ecosystems and UGC strengthens player identification and community stickiness.
- **Definitions:** Implicitly defines a "player co-creation space" as a social media-based channel designed for bidirectional communication and collaborative brand/product development.
- **Dates, Tables, Figures, Metrics, Evidence Types:** None are explicitly provided in this section.

response_text:
This section outlines a "player co-creation" brand operation paradigm for competitive games, detailing strategies to integrate player communities into brand development and marketing. It addresses retrieval queries regarding:
- **Topics & Procedures:** Establishing bidirectional social media communication channels to collect optimization feedback, invite core players into co-creation workflows, and report back on suggestion implementation; and linking community ecosystems with competitive tournaments by launching social media-driven gameplay creation contests, curating high-quality UGC for integration into official channels and events, and recruiting skilled players to produce and disseminate secondary content.
- **Entities:** Social media platforms, general/core/skilled players, UGC creators, official content matrices, tournament ecosystems, and the game brand.
- **Claims:** Maintaining transparent feedback loops and actively involving core players converts the player base into brand co-creators, while integrating community activities with tournament ecosystems and UGC strengthens player identification and community stickiness.
- **Definitions:** Implicitly defines a "player co-creation space" as a social media-based channel designed for bidirectional communication and collaborative brand/product development.
- **Dates, Tables, Figures, Metrics, Evidence Types:** None are explicitly provided in this section.
